channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: At UN climate talks, nations blast draft of vague deal on climate cash for developing countries
item.description: BAKU, Azerbaijan (AP) — Countries of the world took turns rejecting a new but vague draft text released early Thursday which attempts to form the spine of any deal reached at United Nations climate talks on money for developing countries to transition to clean energy and adapt to climate change.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, SIBI ARASU and MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: International Criminal Court issues arrest warrants for Netanyahu and Hamas officials
item.description: The International Criminal Court issued arrest warrants for Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, his former defense minister and Hamas officials, accusing them of war crimes and crimes against humanity over the war in Gaza and the October attacks that triggered Israel’s offensive in the Palest
item.dc:creator: Molly Quell, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Ursula Haverbeck, German far-right activist repeatedly convicted for Holocaust denial, dies at 96
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Ursula Haverbeck, a prominent German far-right activist who accumulated a string of convictions for denying the Holocaust, has died. She was 96. Haverbeck died on Wednesday, her lawyer Wolfram Nahrath told German news agency dpa on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australian tourist dies after drinking tainted alcohol in Laos, raising toll to 4 including American
item.description: VIENTIANE, Laos (AP) — An Australian teenager has died after drinking tainted alcohol in Laos in what Australia’s prime minister on Thursday called every parent’s nightmare.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US to give antipersonnel mines to Ukraine to help slow the Russian advance
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — The Biden administration announced Wednesday that it will give Ukraine antipersonnel mines to help it slow Russia’s battlefield advances, marking the second major shift on U.S. military support for Kyiv in days.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, ILLIA NOVIKOV, MATTHEW LEE and LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 19 Senators back limiting offensive weapons to Israel in key vote
item.description: The Senate on Wednesday failed to pass three resolutions that would have limited offensive arms to Israel, but 19 senators supported the initiative, showing there is growing resistance to the Israeli war against Hamas in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: The dark energy pushing our universe apart may not be what it seems, scientists say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Distant, ancient galaxies are giving scientists more hints that a mysterious force called dark energy may not be what they thought.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis rejects tradition, opts for humble wooden casket and burial outside Vatican
item.description: Pope Francis announced this week that he intends on having a much more simple funeral than those of his predecessors at the Vatican, including a humble wooden casket.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US vetoes UN resolution demanding a cease-fire in Gaza because there's no link to a hostage release
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — The United States on Wednesday vetoed a U.N. Security Council resolution demanding an immediate cease-fire in the war in Gaza because it was not linked to an immediate release of hostages taken captive by Hamas militants in Israel in October 2023.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: US vetoes a Gaza cease-fire resolution
item.description: The United States vetoed a U.N. resolution demanding an immediate cease-fire in the Israel-Hamas war in Gaza, with the Americans saying they the resolution Wednesday was not linked to an immediate release of hostages still held by Hamas.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine launches first UK storm shadow missiles at Russia
item.description: Ukraine for the first time fired British storm shadow missiles into Russia on Wednesday, according to reports from Bloomberg and Reuters.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Zelenskyy answers whether he's willing to cede Crimea, other territory in peace deal
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy tells Fox News that his country "cannot legally acknowledge any occupied territory of Ukraine as Russian.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Doctors Without Borders suspends operations in Haiti
item.description: Doctors Without Borders has suspended its activities in Port-au-Prince following a series of threats by Haiti's police forces, the organization announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Russian lawmakers endorse bill to ban adoptions by gender-transition countries
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia’s upper house of parliament on Wednesday endorsed a bill banning adoption of Russian children by citizens of countries where gender transitioning is legal. The Federation Council also approved bills that outlaw the spread of material that encourages people not to have children.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope to make late Italian teenager Carlo Acutis the first millennial saint on April 27
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis said he will canonize Carlo Acutis next April, setting the eagerly awaited date for the late teenager to become the Catholic Church’s first millennial and digital-era saint.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN accused of downplaying Hamas terrorists' use of Gaza hospitals as new report ignores important details
item.description: IDF reported encountering terrorists in hospitals but Hamas' health ministry refuses to admit the group's presence there. Critics say the U.N. and others are too quick to parrot Hamas talking points.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Marcos says Indonesia has agreed to send Filipino death-row drug convict back to the Philippines
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. said Wednesday that a deal has been reached for Indonesia to send back a Filipino death-row drug convict, who was nearly executed by firing squad but got a reprieve due to years of pleadings from Manila.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ AND NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukrainians’ opinions of US leadership falling: Gallup
item.description: Ukrainians' opinions of the U.S. government leadership have fallen to pre-war levels after reaching a high point in 2022, according to a new Gallup poll released on Wednesday. Ukraine's public opinion of the U.S. leadership fluctuated from 20 percent to 40 percent between 2014 and 2021.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Italian village offers 1-euro homes to Americans wanting to relocate after election
item.description: A small rural village in Italy is offering homes for as little as $1.06 after the 2024 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Ukraine fires several US-made longer-range missiles into Russia for the first time
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine fired several American-supplied longer-range missiles into Russia, officials said Tuesday, marking the first time Kyiv used the weapons that way in 1,000 days of war.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: 1,000 days of war in Ukraine as Zelenskyy doubles down on aerial options with ATACMS, drones and missiles
item.description: Ukraine marks 1,000 days of war with Russia on Tuesday as President Zelenskyy eyes plan to develop thousands of long-range drones and military targets inside Russia using US-supplied ATACMS.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US allies accuse Russia of 'escalating hybrid activities' against NATO, EU nations after data cables severed
item.description: U.S. allies accuse Russia of "escalating hybrid activities" against NATO and EU countries after two data cables were severed in the Baltic Sea this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden's Ukraine missile move will have 'some impact': Retired major
item.description: Lyons questioned the timing of the decision, saying, “I’m not sure why we didn’t allow this two or three years ago.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, The Hill

item.title: 45 pro-democracy Hong Kong activists sentenced to up to 10 years in prison under China-backed law
item.description: Western countries are outraged after Hong Kong used a Chinese national security law to sentence 45 pro-democracy activists to up to 10 years in prison.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bolton: Moscow, Beijing should feel ‘a lot of relief’ if Gabbard is confirmed as DNI
item.description: Former national security adviser John Bolton said that Russia and China should feel “a lot of relief” if former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii) is confirmed as President-elect Trump’s choice for director of national intelligence. “She’s not capable of doing it,” Bolton said Sunday on CNN.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Thune threatens International Criminal Court with sanctions if it doesn’t drop Netanyahu warrant for arrest
item.description: Sen. John Thune said he will go after the International Criminal Court with sanctions once the GOP take the majority in the Senate come January if the court does not drop its case against Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australian zoo asks citizens to catch deadly spiders
item.description: Despite the zany ask, there is a valiant reason behind the call from The Australian Reptile Park in Sydney, New South Wales.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Wreckage of World War II warship found 80 years later
item.description: The USS Edsall, a World War II American warship, was bombed and sank three months after the attack on Pearl Harbor.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Israel's warfare in Gaza 'consistent with genocide:' UN special committee
item.description: Israel's destruction of vital water, sanitation, and food systems has created a "lethal mix of crises," the report said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation
